audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot tabernacle instruction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for bronze altar, courtyard, screen, oil for lamp, and continual light.

verses.restored: You shall make altar of acacia wood, five cubits length and five cubits width; altar shall be square, and three cubits its height.
Make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be from it, and overlay it with bronze.
Make its pots to remove ashes, its shovels, basins, forks, and firepans; all its vessels you shall make bronze.
Make for it a grating, network of bronze, and on net make four bronze rings on its four ends.
Put it under ledge of altar from below, so net reaches halfway up altar.
Make poles for altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze.
Its poles shall be brought into rings, and poles shall be on two sides of altar when carrying it.
Hollow with boards you shall make it; as he showed you on mountain, so they shall make.
You shall make court of dwelling-place: for south side, hangings for court of fine twisted linen, one hundred cubits length for one side.
Its twenty pillars and twenty bases shall be bronze; hooks of pillars and their bands silver.
Likewise for north side in length, hangings one hundred cubits, its twenty pillars and twenty bases bronze; hooks of pillars and bands silver.
Width of court on west side shall be hangings fifty cubits, their ten pillars and ten bases.
Width of court on east side, toward sunrise, fifty cubits.
Hangings for one shoulder shall be fifteen cubits, their three pillars and three bases.
For second shoulder, hangings fifteen cubits, their three pillars and three bases.
For gate of court, screen of twenty cubits, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, work of embroiderer; their four pillars and four bases.
All pillars of court around shall be banded with silver, their hooks silver, their bases bronze.
Length of court shall be one hundred cubits, width fifty by fifty, height five cubits, fine twisted linen, and their bases bronze.
All vessels of dwelling-place for all its service, all its pegs, and all pegs of court shall be bronze.
And you shall command children of Israel, and they shall bring to you pure beaten olive oil for light, to make lamp go up continually.

In tent of meeting, outside veil that is before testimony, Aaron and his sons shall arrange it from evening to morning before YHWH; an age-long statute throughout their generations from children of Israel.

translation_notes: Bronze altar and court continue portable sacred service architecture.
Repeated poles/portable design show wilderness mobility.
Lamp oil comes from Israel and is tended continually; communal provision sustains sacred light.
Even technical measurements carry ordered access and boundary rather than spectacle.
safety_note: Do not treat courtyard boundaries as elitist exclusion detached from holiness; the text is arranging safe, ordered approach to presence.
